name: Fig Star Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the shortening and granulated sugar. Beat in the corn syrup, maple flavoring, and beaten eggs.
2. Sift together the all-purpose flour and salt; stir into the shortening mixture.
3. Shape the dough into two rolls about 2-1/2 inches in diameter. Wrap in plastic wrap and chill for several hours.
4. For the filling: Cover the dried figs with boiling water and let stand for 10 minutes. Drain, clip off the stems, and cut fairly fine with scissors. Add the cold water and cook for about 10 minutes until tender. Stir in the corn syrup and lemon rind; cook for a few minutes longer.
5. Mix the granulated sugar, flour, and salt. Add all at once to the fig mixture and cook, stirring, until thickened and clear. Remove from heat and stir in the lemon juice and chopped nuts.
6. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
7. For star cookies: Cut the chilled dough into thin slices. Place a small spoonful of fig filling in the center of each slice. Fold up the edges and pinch together to form a rough star shape.
8. For filled cookies: Stack two cookie slices with a dot of fig filling between them. Press the edges together and prick the top with a fork or mark with a knife.
9. Bake either type of cookie for 8-10 minutes, or until delicately browned around the edges. Cool on wire racks.
Makes about 3 dozen filled cookies or 6 dozen star cookies.
